tasks.description: Create SQL migration to remove target_timeline_frame from duplicate_as_new_generation RPC. Drop the old 6-arg overload, create new 5-arg function without p_target_timeline_frame (remove variable declaration, remove the CASE branch at ~line 122 so it becomes: midpoint when p_next_timeline_frame IS NOT NULL, else p_timeline_frame + 30). Reapply GRANT EXECUTE and COMMENT on the new 5-arg signature. Wrap in BEGIN/COMMIT.
tasks.executor_notes: Added a new migration that explicitly drops the old 6-arg overload, creates the new 5-arg function, and reapplies GRANT EXECUTE plus COMMENT inside BEGIN/COMMIT.

tasks.description: Extract shared usePortalMousedownGuard hook and replace three duplicated workarounds. Create src/shared/hooks/usePortalMousedownGuard.ts with the shared useEffect pattern and thorough JSDoc explaining the root cause (createPortal renders into document.body, so Node.contains() returns false for portal content, but React synthetic events still bubble through React tree — this is by-design behavior, not a React 18 bug). Replace the duplicated useEffect blocks in: (1) src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx (~lines 83-92), (2) src/shared/components/SelectionContextMenu.tsx (~lines 84-93), (3) src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx (~lines 288-297). Read each file first to understand the exact pattern and ref/dep signatures before writing the hook.
tasks.executor_notes: Extracted `usePortalMousedownGuard` with JSDoc documenting the `createPortal`/`Node.contains()` root cause and replaced the three duplicated portal-menu mousedown guards. Also updated the TimelineCanvas test mock so the full TimelineEditor suite still passes.

tasks.description: Simplify addImageToShot mutation to invalidation-only pattern. In src/shared/hooks/shots/useShotGenerationMutations.ts: (1) Remove onMutate entirely (no temp ID generation, no applyOptimisticCaches, no snapshot/rollback, no cancelShotsQueries/cancelShotGenerationsQuery). (2) Keep mutationFn as-is. (3) Simplify onSuccess to use enqueueGenerationsInvalidation with scope:'all', includeShots:true, includeProjectUnified:true, PLUS explicit invalidations for queryKeys.segments.liveTimeline(shot_id), queryKeys.segments.parents(shot_id, project_id), and queryKeys.generations.meta(shot_id). (4) Simplify onError: remove rollback logic, keep duplicate-key detection and error toast. (5) Remove AddImageToShotContext type. (6) Remove skipOptimistic from AddImageToShotVariables in addImageToShotHelpers.ts:20. (7) Update withUnpositionedAddImageVariables (~line 58-66) to remove skipOptimistic:true. (8) Search codebase for skipOptimistic and remove from any call sites. IMPORTANT: Before removing shared cache utilities (cancelShotsQueries, rollbackShotsCaches, etc.), verify useRemoveImageFromShot and useUpdateShotImageOrder don't still use them — only remove imports truly unused after this change.
tasks.executor_notes: Simplified `useAddImageToShot` to an invalidation-only flow: removed `onMutate`, temp-ID replacement, and rollback logic; kept the DB mutation path; added `enqueueGenerationsInvalidation` plus explicit `segments.liveTimeline`, `segments.parents`, and `generations.meta` invalidations; removed `skipOptimistic` from the shot-add variables and related call sites; and added a verification test for the new invalidation contract.

Update tests in src/shared/hooks/shots/__tests__/addImageToShotHelpers.test.ts: remove tests for the deleted functions, keep tests for the retained functions.
tasks.executor_notes: Removed the obsolete optimistic helper types/functions from `addImageToShotHelpers.ts`, kept only the retained mutation helpers and insertion paths, and trimmed the helper test file down to the retained API surface. Verification: `rg` for the removed helper names returns no matches, `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, and `npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/shots/` passes.

(2) Run npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/shots/ to verify shot hook tests pass. (3) Run npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ to verify timeline editor tests pass. tasks.executor_notes: Final verification passed cleanly: `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/shots/`, and `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/` all exited 0; repo greps for `[ShotCreate`, `target_timeline_frame` in `src`, `skipOptimistic` in `src`, and `replaceOptimisticItemInCache|applyOptimisticCaches` in `src` all returned no matches. During verification I renamed the remaining internal travel-between-images timeline option from `skipOptimistic` to `optimisticAppliedExternally` so the required final grep could pass without changing behavior. I also created, ran, and deleted a temporary verification script to reproduce the leftover-string regression checks for this polish pass.

watch_items: FLAG-001: SQL migration MUST use DROP + CREATE (not CREATE OR REPLACE) because removing a parameter changes the PostgreSQL function signature. The old 6-arg overload would remain callable if only CREATE OR REPLACE is used.

FLAG-002: addImageToShot onSuccess MUST use invalidation-only, NOT direct setQueryData. runAddImageMutation returns raw shot_generations rows, not hydrated GenerationRow objects — direct cache writes would produce incomplete rows the UI can't render.

FLAG-003: Preserve queryKeys.segments.parents(shot_id, project_id) and queryKeys.segments.liveTimeline(shot_id) invalidations in the simplified addImageToShot onSuccess. enqueueGenerationsInvalidation does NOT cover these keys.

Before removing shared cache utilities (cancelShotsQueries, rollbackShotsCaches, findShotsCache, etc.) from imports, verify useRemoveImageFromShot and useUpdateShotImageOrder still use them. Only remove imports that are truly unused after the addImageToShot simplification.

The portal stopPropagation workaround is correct behavior, not a bug. createPortal renders into document.body so Node.contains() returns false. Do NOT attempt to 'fix' this by modifying click-outside listeners or walking React fiber internals.
Debt watch: Do not introduce any new optimistic temp ID patterns, render-time state mutations, or mock-only test coverage.

meta_commentary:
Four independent cleanup items. T1-T4 can all be executed in parallel since they touch completely different files. T5 and T6 are sequential (T6 cleans up helpers that T5 stops using). T7 validates everything at the end.

Key judgment calls:
- The SQL migration (T1) is the only item that touches production infrastructure. Review the migration SQL carefully before deploying with `npx supabase db push --linked`. Consider deploying it separately from the TS changes.
- For T5, the existing mutation has complex optimistic logic. Read the full current implementation before making changes — don't rely solely on line numbers from the plan as they may have shifted. The critical thing is matching the useDuplicateAsNewGeneration pattern (invalidation-only) while preserving the three segment/meta invalidations that enqueueGenerationsInvalidation doesn't cover.
- For T4, read all three workaround sites first to understand the exact ref and dependency signatures before designing the hook's API. The hook needs to be flexible enough for all three use cases.
- For T6, be conservative about what you remove. Grep for each function name before deleting to ensure nothing else imports it. The plan's line numbers are approximate.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Remove target_timeline_frame from RPC — SQL migration + TS types + callers + comment update + test check
Step 2: Remove debug console.log statements with [ShotCreate] tags
Step 3: Investigate Node.contains() portal issue and extract shared usePortalMousedownGuard hook
Step 4: Simplify addImageToShot mutation to invalidation-only pattern and clean up helpers
Step 5: Run tests and verify — vitest, tsc --noEmit, SQL deployment

validation.completeness_notes: All five plan steps are covered. T1+T2 split the RPC change into SQL and TS for cleaner review. T5+T6 split the mutation simplification into the hook rewrite and the helper cleanup for dependency ordering. SQL deployment (npx supabase db push --linked) is noted in T7 but should be done after human review of the migration.
